_note: Sector correlation library for tradebrain. Leader = ticker that moves first and drags the sector. Beta = rough move multiplier vs leader. Correlated_sectors = sectors that tend to move together.

sectors.name: Semiconductors - compute
sectors.leader_note: NVDA is the anchor for the entire AI ecosystem. NVDA earnings/guidance moves the whole sector.
sectors.risk_note: NVDA earnings weeks cause IV spikes across entire sector. Avoid buying options into NVDA earnings unless trading the event itself.

sectors.name: Semiconductors - memory
sectors.leader_note: MU leads the memory cycle. Currently rotating ahead of GPU stocks as HBM demand tightens supply.
sectors.risk_note: Memory cycle is currently decoupled from GPU cycle - MU up does not always mean NVDA up. Treat as separate trade.

sectors.name: Semiconductors - equipment
sectors.leader_note: AMAT and LRCX lead equipment. ASML is the global monopoly on EUV lithography - its moves signal the entire fab buildout.
sectors.risk_note: Equipment stocks lag compute by 1-2 quarters. Positive NVDA guidance = future AMAT order tailwind, not immediate move.

sectors.name: AI infrastructure and data centers
sectors.leader_note: NBIS leads the high-beta AI infra cluster. CRWV and APLD are the next highest beta. All share power/energy cost as primary driver.
sectors.risk_note: Energy prices are a direct cost driver - oil/gas down = AI infra up. This cluster moves 2-3x the broader market on risk-on days. Very high IV, wide spreads. Size carefully.

sectors.name: Big Tech - hyperscalers
sectors.leader_note: MSFT leads because Azure/OpenAI capex signals the demand side for the entire AI ecosystem. GOOGL and META follow. AAPL is more defensive and less correlated to AI.
sectors.risk_note: Hyperscaler capex guidance (Azure, AWS, GCP growth %) is the single most important data point for the entire AI trade. Watch quarterly earnings closely.

sectors.name: AI software and productivity
sectors.leader_note: PLTR leads sentiment for the AI software category. CRM and SOUN follow. This sector lags the hardware cycle by 2-4 quarters.
sectors.risk_note: AI software stocks are sentiment-driven and can disconnect from fundamentals. High short interest on most names = squeeze risk on positive catalysts.

sectors.name: Cloud and cybersecurity
sectors.leader_note: CRWD leads the cyber cluster. PANW is the large-cap anchor. ZS and FTNT follow. Cyber is more defensive than pure AI plays.
sectors.risk_note: Cybersecurity is more defensive - tends to hold up better in risk-off moves. Budget cycles and breach headlines are primary catalysts.

sectors.name: Industrial automation and robotics
sectors.leader_note: Robotics is early-stage with no dominant leader yet. PATH and SYM are the most liquid. SERV is speculative.
sectors.risk_note: Very thin liquidity on most names. Options spreads are wide. Stick to PATH and SYM if trading options - avoid SERV options entirely.

sectors.name: Healthcare and healthtech
sectors.leader_note: HIMS leads the high-growth healthtech cluster. OSCR follows. UNH is a large-cap traditional insurer - inverse correlation to HIMS on policy news.
sectors.risk_note: UNH and HIMS can move inversely on healthcare policy news. Treat as separate trades. HIMS is a momentum name - high IV, moves fast.

sectors.name: Crypto miners and power plays
sectors.leader_note: MARA leads crypto miner sentiment. RIOT follows. BITF is more leveraged to BTC. All three correlate with BTC price AND power costs - same dual driver as AI infra.
sectors.risk_note: BTC price is the primary driver. Secondary driver is power costs and regulatory news. Very high IV - buying options is expensive. Consider selling premium instead.

sectors.name: Quantum computing
sectors.leader_note: IONQ is now the most liquid and institutionally backed quantum name. RGTI and QBTS follow. All move on quantum news and NVIDIA comments about AI+quantum convergence.
sectors.risk_note: Pure speculation tier. Revenue is real but small. Move on press releases, conference mentions, and big-tech quantum comments. IONQ is the most liquid - use it as the proxy for the others. Never more than 1 contract. Treat as lottery tickets with a real thesis.

sectors.name: Broad market ETFs
sectors.leader_note: SPY is the macro anchor. QQQ leads tech. IWM leads small caps and is a risk appetite indicator. When IWM lags QQQ, risk appetite is narrowing.
sectors.risk_note: QQQ/SPY ratio is a useful tech sentiment gauge. QQQ outperforming = risk-on tech. QQQ underperforming = rotation to value.

sectors.name: Energy infrastructure - new
sectors.leader_note: SMR leads the nuclear/clean energy plays for AI power demand. EOSE is the battery storage angle. Both benefit from data center power demand.
sectors.risk_note: Nuclear and energy storage plays benefit from the same AI power demand thesis as data centers. Less volatile than AI infra - better for defined-risk plays.

sectors.name: Cybersecurity - next gen
sectors.leader_note: CRWD leads next-gen cyber. S (SentinelOne) is the closest competitor with a differentiated AI-native platform.
sectors.risk_note: Cybersecurity is defensive but S specifically has had fundamentals-vs-price disconnect. Good long-term story, weak near-term momentum. Buy thesis needs a catalyst - earnings beat, contract win, or sector rotation into cyber.

sectors.name: Data infrastructure and AI data
sectors.leader_note: PLTR leads the AI data and analytics category. INOD is a niche pure-play in data annotation for AI training - different model but same demand driver.
sectors.risk_note: INOD is micro-cap with thin options liquidity. Options spreads will be wide. Treat any INOD options play as speculative - size at 1 contract maximum and expect relaxed filter picks. PLTR is the liquid proxy.

sectors.name: Fintech and financial platforms
sectors.leader_note: COIN leads fintech sentiment. HOOD follows retail trading volume. SOFI is rate-sensitive.
sectors.risk_note: Sensitive to crypto prices, interest rates, and retail trading volume

sectors.name: EV and Auto
sectors.leader_note: TSLA leads EV sentiment globally. RIVN and NIO follow with higher beta. GM/F are traditional auto proxies with lower beta.
sectors.risk_note: EV names are macro-sensitive and policy-driven. Tariff news, EV incentive changes, and TSLA delivery data are primary catalysts. NIO and RIVN have thin margins — any guidance miss hits hard.

sectors.name: Social media and consumer internet
sectors.leader_note: META leads social/consumer internet. SNAP and PINS are high-beta followers. Ad market conditions drive the whole group.
sectors.risk_note: Ad spend is cyclical — recession fears compress the whole group fast. SNAP is the highest beta name relative to META. Earnings weeks cause IV spikes across the cluster.

sectors.name: China ADRs
sectors.leader_note: BABA leads China ADR sentiment. PDD and JD follow. Geopolitical headlines and US-China trade policy are the primary macro drivers.
sectors.risk_note: China ADRs can gap 5-10% on single headlines — tariffs, delisting threats, regulatory crackdowns. Always check geopolitical calendar before entering. High IV is normal — options are expensive.

sectors.name: Traditional energy
sectors.leader_note: XOM leads traditional energy. OXY and CVX follow. Oil price and production data are the primary drivers.
sectors.risk_note: Oil macro drives the sector — WTI price, OPEC decisions, inventory data. Lower volatility than AI names. Better for defined-risk plays with wider DTE.

cross_sector_signals.note: NVDA up = entire AI stack bid. Strongest same-day correlation to AMD, AVGO. Data center stocks follow within 1-2 sessions.
MSFT Azure guidance = demand signal for the whole cloud and AI software stack.
Broad market risk-on lifts high-beta AI names disproportionately. QQQ typically leads SPY on tech-driven days.
AI data center cluster moves together on energy cost and hyperscaler demand news.
Memory cycle move. Currently semi-decoupled from GPU compute - MU up is a memory-specific signal, not a broad semi signal.
